catalog.label: Catalog
catalog.hint: Pre-built servers you can register with one click

bundles.label: Bundles
bundles.hint: Curated tool selections to expose to clients

keys.label: API keys
keys.hint: MCP bearer tokens issued to consumers

teams.label: Teams
teams.hint: Group users to scope policies and ownership

sidebar.brand_live_title: Live traffic in the last minute

connect_client_dialog.title: Connect a client
connect_client_dialog.hint: Generate a ready-to-paste MCP connection config for this gateway. Nothing here is saved or sent anywhere — it's assembled entirely in your browser.
connect_client_dialog.key_warning: You don't have an active MCP API key yet.
connect_client_dialog.key_warning_after: first, then come back and paste it in below.
connect_client_dialog.key_count: You have {count} active MCP API key(s) — paste one in place of
connect_client_dialog.choose_target: Choose a {kind} above to generate its config.

share_install_link.title: Share install link
share_install_link.hint_p1: Anyone with this link can connect to
share_install_link.hint_p2: immediately — no admin login required. It carries a fresh MCP API key scoped ONLY to this bundle's tools, never your own credentials. Revoke a link at any time to cut off access instantly.
share_install_link.minted_title: New link created — copy it now, the token won't be shown again:
share_install_link.empty: No install links yet for this bundle.

confirm.revoke_message: Anyone using this link (or its embedded key) will lose access immediately. This cannot be undone.

bundle_tool_picker.filter_placeholder: Filter by client, tool, or description…

empty.no_tools: No tools available yet — register a client first.

server_detail_upstream_auth.title: Upstream authentication
server_detail_upstream_auth.not_configured: Not configured — requests to this backend are sent without credentials.
server_detail_upstream_auth.oauth_note: Alternative to Upstream OAuth below — both can be set at once, but if so the OAuth2 bearer token wins the Authorization header on outbound calls.

confirm.clear_message: This removes the stored credentials for this backend. This can't be undone — requests will be sent without credentials until you set new ones.

server_detail_oauth.title: Upstream OAuth (client credentials)
server_detail_oauth.not_configured: Not configured — the bridge mints a token via client-credentials before each call and injects it as Authorization: Bearer …. The client secret is write-only and never shown again once saved.

confirm.clear_message: This removes the stored token URL, client ID, and client secret. This can't be undone — outbound calls will stop injecting an OAuth bearer token until you set new credentials.

errors.required_fields: Token URL, client ID, and client secret are required.

server_detail_resync.rediscover_hint: Re-connects to {url} and refreshes this server's tool list.

server_detail_lb.title: Load balancing
server_detail_lb.hint: Spread calls across the primary backend plus an N-way pool of additional targets. Takes precedence over canary/failover below — while an enabled pool has at least one enabled target, canary routing is skipped entirely for this server.
server_detail_lb.empty_targets: No pool targets yet — add one below.

confirm.clear_message: This removes the pool strategy config for this server, but keeps the individual targets stored — routing falls back to the primary backend only (and canary/failover, if configured) until you reconfigure the pool.
confirm.remove_message: '{url}' will stop receiving traffic immediately. If it's actively serving calls, in-flight requests to it are unaffected but no new ones will be routed there.

errors.weight_invalid: Weight must be a whole number of at least 1.

server_detail_canary.title: Canary / failover
server_detail_canary.hint: Route to a secondary backend. canary: send a % of calls there; failover: route there only while the primary breaker is open.
server_detail_canary.note: Note: Load balancing above takes precedence — when an enabled pool has at least one enabled target, this canary/failover config is bypassed.

confirm.clear_message: This removes the secondary backend routing configuration. This can't be undone — you'll need to reconfigure it to restore this setup.

server_detail_remove.title: Remove server
server_detail_remove.hint: Unlike Disable above, this permanently deletes the server's registration, guards, and all per-tool configuration. Connected MCP agents lose access to its tools immediately, and this can't be undone — Disable above is the reversible alternative.

confirm.title: Remove this server?
confirm.message: This permanently deletes the registration, guards, and all per-tool configuration for '{name}'. Connected MCP agents lose access to its tools immediately. This can't be undone.

server_detail_tools.heading: Tools ({count})
server_detail_tools.empty: This server has no tools registered.

confirm.disable_message: '{name}' will stop working for all connected MCP agents until re-enabled.

server_detail_playground.title: Playground
server_detail_playground.hint: Fill arguments from the tool's schema and run a real test call through the full guard stack.
server_detail_playground.save_aria: Save the current run as a named example

guard_editor.no_restriction: No restriction — any valid MCP key may call this tool.
guard_editor.keys_hashed_hint: Keys are hashed on save; existing keys cannot be displayed again.
guard_editor.add_key_placeholder: Paste a raw API key to add
guard_editor.replace_warning: Saving will REPLACE the entire allow-list with these {count} key(s).

confirm.clear_title: Clear all guards for this tool?
confirm.clear_message: This removes the rate limit, timeout override, and API key allow-list. Existing keys are hashed and cannot be restored — you'd need the original raw keys to set the same allow-list again.

guard_editor_approval.title: Human-in-the-loop approval
guard_editor_approval.require_label: Require human approval before this tool runs
guard_editor_approval.levels_hint: A call is only allowed once this many DIFFERENT admins/operators have approved it (1 = today's single-approval behavior). Any single rejection blocks the call immediately, regardless of prior approvals.
guard_editor_approval.levels_error: Must be a whole number between 1 and 10

guard_editor_cache_purge.title: Response cache
guard_editor_cache_purge.hint: Clears any responses already cached for this tool. Doesn't change the cache's enabled/TTL config — new responses are cached again on the next matching call.

guard_editor_coalesce.title: Request coalescing
guard_editor_coalesce.label: Share one upstream fetch across concurrent identical calls (GET tools only)
guard_editor_coalesce.hint: Distinct from the response cache's TTL — only dedupes calls that are in flight at the same moment, so it's safe even without caching enabled.

guard_editor_graphql.title: GraphQL backend
guard_editor_graphql.enable_label: Dispatch this tool as a GraphQL query/mutation instead of a plain REST body
guard_editor_graphql.hint_p1: Tool-call arguments are sent as GraphQL variables — declare a
guard_editor_graphql.hint_p2: for each argument this tool's input schema accepts. Auto-discovered tools start with a synthesized query you can extend here (e.g. deeper selection sets).

guard_editor_guardrails.title: Guardrails
guard_editor_guardrails.content_hint: Input deny patterns (one regex per line). A call whose arguments match any pattern is rejected before dispatch.
guard_editor_guardrails.block_secrets: Block arguments that look like secrets (AWS keys, private keys, tokens…)
guard_editor_guardrails.scan_responses: Scan responses for prompt-injection and wrap flagged output

guard_editor_presentation.title: Presentation
guard_editor_presentation.name_hint_p1: Renames the tool for MCP clients. The
guard_editor_presentation.name_hint_p2: prefix is always kept. Leave blank to use the registered name.
guard_editor_presentation.desc_hint: Replaces what MCP clients see for this tool in tools/list. Leave blank to use the registered description.
guard_editor_presentation.desc_placeholder: Registered description is used when blank

errors.save_failed: Failed to save the presentation override.

guard_editor_redaction.title: Redaction
guard_editor_redaction.label: Response redaction paths
guard_editor_redaction.hint: One dot-path per line (e.g. user.ssn, items.*.secret). Matching JSON values are replaced with [REDACTED] before returning to the caller.

guard_editor_tags.title: Tags
guard_editor_tags.label: Comma-separated tags
guard_editor_tags.hint: Comma-separated (lowercase letters, digits, - and _). Used to organize and filter tools.

guard_editor_websocket.title: WebSocket backend
guard_editor_websocket.enable_label: Dispatch this tool over a WebSocket instead of REST
guard_editor_websocket.url_error: Must start with ws:// or wss://
guard_editor_websocket.persistent_label: Persistent connection — forward every message as progress instead of closing after the first
guard_editor_websocket.hint: Non-persistent (default) opens a fresh connection per call and returns the first message. Persistent stays open and resolves with the last message once the connection closes or the timeout elapses — intermediate messages are forwarded as MCP progress notifications to callers that requested them.

guard_editor_context_budget.title: Context budget
guard_editor_context_budget.enable_label: Cap this tool's response size so it can't blow an agent's context window
guard_editor_context_budget.hint: No budget configured (the default) means responses are returned unbounded, exactly as today. Once enabled, a response over the limit is either cut off with a marker, or — if you configure an LLM below — compressed into a faithful summary instead.
guard_editor_context_budget.bytes_error: Must be a whole number of at least 256 bytes
guard_editor_context_budget.mode_label: Mode when a response exceeds the limit

mode.truncate: Truncate — cut it off and note how much was omitted
mode.llm: Compress with an LLM — summarize instead of cutting off

guard_editor_context_budget.llm_hint: One extra call per oversized response, made only after this tool's own redaction and guardrail scan have already run — the LLM never sees pre-redaction data. Any failure (network, non-2xx, timeout) silently falls back to truncation; the tool call itself never fails because of this.
guard_editor_context_budget.api_key_hint_configured: A key is already configured and is write-only — it cannot be displayed again. Saving here, even just to change another field, replaces it, so re-enter the key.
guard_editor_context_budget.api_key_hint_new: Bring your own key — stored encrypted, never displayed again once saved.

guard_editor_quarantine.title: Auto-quarantine
guard_editor_quarantine.threshold_error: Must be a whole number between 1 and 100

action.block: Block calls (same as disabling the tool)
action.force_approval: Force every call through human approval
action.observe: Observe only — log and let calls through

recovery.manual: Manual only — an admin must clear it
recovery.auto: Automatic — clears itself after a cooldown

guard_editor_quarantine.cooldown_positive: Must be a positive number of minutes

add_widget.title: Add a widget
add_widget.hint: Pick a metric to add to your dashboard. Everything you add can be resized, moved, or removed.

config_import.title: Import
config_import.hint: Paste an exported document (policy-as-code — includes guardrails and consumer quotas). Dry-run first to preview what would change — client/tool config only applies to already-registered servers.

confirm.title: Apply this import?
confirm.message: This overwrites bundles, alert rules, and per-client/tool configuration on already-registered servers with the contents of the pasted document. This cannot be undone from here.

config_snapshots.title: Version history
config_snapshots.hint: Snapshot the current config, diff a snapshot against the live config, or roll back to it.
config_snapshots.empty: No snapshots yet. A snapshot captures the full config -- bundles, guardrails, consumers, and more -- so you can compare or roll back later.

confirm.rollback_message: Roll back config to snapshot '{label}'? This re-applies it to existing servers.

schema_form.json_invalid: Invalid JSON — sent as a raw string instead.

demo_ribbon.label: Live demo — data is mocked · View source ↗

mobile_topbar.live_traffic: Live traffic in the last minute

widgets.note_default_text:
# Notes

Write anything here — **markdown** and https://links work.

descriptions.timeseries: Line + area over the selected time window
descriptions.note: A free-text markdown panel for annotations and links

servers.title: Servers
servers.subtitle: Registered backend servers and their tools.

aria.select_all: Select all servers on this page

empty.tags: No tools have been tagged yet. Tag a tool from its server's Settings tab.
empty.no_servers: No servers registered yet. REST backends register themselves via POST /register; you can also add a REST or MCP server manually.

confirm.disable_message: Disabling '{name}' will stop all {tools} of its tools for every connected MCP agent.
confirm.bulk_disable_message: Disabling {count} server(s) will stop all of their tools for every connected MCP agent.

errors.tag_tools_load_failed: Failed to load tools for this tag.

login.subtitle: Sign in to manage servers and tools
login.invalid_credentials: Couldn't sign in — check your username and password.
login.too_many_attempts: Too many attempts — please wait a moment and try again.
login.generic_error: Something went wrong. Please try again.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: A live snapshot of this bridge — build it out of the widgets you care about.
overview.load_error: {count} data source(s) couldn't load — some widgets may be empty. Try refreshing.

empty.edit_hint: Drag the ⠿ handle to move a widget, drag its bottom-right corner to resize, or use each widget's controls. Changes are saved automatically to this browser.

reset_confirm.title: Reset dashboard?
reset_confirm.message: This replaces your current layout with the default set of widgets. Your customizations to this dashboard will be lost.

bundles.title: Bundles
bundles.subtitle: Cross-client tool selections, each served at its own /mcp-custom/{name} endpoint.

empty.no_bundles: No bundles yet. A bundle lets you hand an MCP client a curated, cross-client tool selection instead of one client's full tool list.

new.title: Create bundle
new.subtitle_p1: Cross-client tool selections, each served at its own

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this bundle. Leaving now will discard them.

keys.title: API keys
keys.subtitle: MCP keys authenticate clients calling the bridge. Scope a key to specific clients or tools, or leave it unrestricted. The secret is shown only once at creation.
keys.empty: No API keys yet. MCP clients present a key to call tools through this bridge — mint one to get started.

confirm.delete_message: '{label}' will stop working immediately and be removed permanently.
confirm.revoke_message: '{label}' will stop working immediately. Revoking cannot be undone.

new.title: Mint API key
new.minted_title: New key "{label}" — copy it now, it won't be shown again:

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this API key. Leaving now will discard them.

register_server.title: Register a server
register_server.base_url_label: Base URL (optional — defaults to the health URL's origin)
register_server.manual_tools_label: Tools (JSON array describing name, method, endpoint, description, inputSchema per tool)
register_server.curl_hint: Paste one or more cURL commands — separate multiple with a blank line, or use a trailing "\" to continue one command across lines. An optional "# name" comment line right above a command sets that tool's name.
register_server.graphql_health_label: Health URL (optional — defaults to the GraphQL URL)
register_server.graphql_health_hint: Many GraphQL servers reject a bare GET on the operation endpoint. If you have a dedicated liveness endpoint, use it here to avoid false health-check failures.
register_server.mcp_transport_hint: The bridge connects to the MCP server and discovers its tools on registration. If the server requires authentication, register it first, set upstream credentials on its detail page, then re-discover.
register_server.preview_hint: Run Preview tools first so you can confirm what will be registered.
register_server.preview_stale_hint: Preview is out of date — run it again before registering.

errors.manual_tools_required: Enter a tools JSON array first.
errors.postman_required: Paste or upload a Postman collection first.
errors.name_and_mcp_required: Name and MCP server URL are required.
errors.name_and_graphql_required: Name and GraphQL URL are required.
errors.name_and_health_required: Name and health URL are required.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved registration details for this server. Leaving now will discard them.

account.title: Account
account.subtitle: Manage your password and active sessions for {username}.
account.change_password_warning: Changing your password immediately signs out every other active session.
account.active_sessions_subtitle: Devices and browsers currently signed in as you. Sign out any you don't recognize.
account.active_sessions_hint: This list doesn't indicate which session is the device you're using right now. Revoking a session may sign that device out immediately.
account.density_hint: Affects Traffic, Audit log, Traces, and API keys.
account.locale_hint: Affects this admin UI only — backend errors and CLI output stay in English.

success.password_changed: Password changed. You're still signed in on this device.

errors.password_too_short: New password must be at least 12 characters.
errors.password_mismatch: New password and confirmation do not match.

revoke_confirm.title: Sign out this device?
revoke_confirm.message: This immediately revokes the session. If it's the device you're using right now, you'll be signed out right away and returned to the login page.

audit_log.title: Audit log
audit_log.subtitle: Who changed what, and when.
audit_log.integrity_broken: Tampering detected — chain breaks at entry #{id} (after {count} valid). Escalate to security and do not trust entries after this point.

alerts.title: Alerts
alerts.subtitle: Webhook alert rules for tool failures, traffic spikes and more.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this alert rule. Leaving now will discard them.

alerts.new_subtitle: Rules are evaluated on the leader instance and POST a JSON payload to a webhook when a condition first becomes true.

errors_create.threshold_invalid: Threshold must be a plain number.
errors_create.min_calls_invalid: Minimum calls must be a plain number.

empty.nothing_pending: Nothing waiting for review right now.
empty.no_items_for_tab: No {tab} approvals yet. Requests show up here once a tool is configured to require approval before its upstream call runs.

confirm.approve_message: This will let {client}'s pending call to {tool} run immediately. This cannot be undone.
confirm.reject_message: This will deny {client}'s pending call to {tool}. This cannot be undone.

composites.title: Composites
composites.subtitle: Composite tools — each one chains several tool calls into a single MCP-callable.

empty.no_composites: No composite tools yet. A composite chains multiple tool calls so a single MCP request triggers a sequence.

confirm.delete_message: MCP clients calling '{name}' will start failing immediately. This cannot be undone.

new.title: New composite
new.subtitle_p1: Chains several existing tool calls into one. Not reachable until added to a bundle's composites — each step forwards to a real

errors.name_format: Lowercase letters, digits, - and _ (max 63 chars); must not contain '__'.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this composite. Leaving now will discard them.

confirm.delete_message: MCP clients calling '{name}' will start failing immediately. This cannot be undone.
confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved changes to the description, input schema, or steps for this composite. Leaving now will discard them.

confirm.delete_message: Delete bundle “{name}”? Tools themselves stay registered — only the curated selection goes away.
confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved tool selections. Leaving now will discard them.

errors.reset_breaker_failed: Failed to reset the circuit breaker.

server_detail.tool_not_found: Tool not found on this server.

confirm.disable_message: Disable {name}? Its tools will stop being advertised until you re-enable it.

catalog.title: Catalog
catalog.subtitle: Curated server templates you can install with one click.

empty.no_entries: No catalog entries yet. The catalog lists one-click installable servers — built-in or admin-added — so registering a new backend doesn't start from a blank form.

confirm.delete_message: '{name}' will be removed from the catalog. This does not affect any servers already installed from it.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this catalog entry. Leaving now will discard them.

config.title: Config
config.subtitle: Export, import and snapshot the live bridge configuration.

consumers.title: Consumers
consumers.subtitle: Consumers (teams / apps) own API keys and can carry a monthly call quota and an optional per-end-user rate limit enforced across all their keys.

empty.no_consumers: No consumers yet. A consumer groups one or more API keys under a shared monthly quota and rate limit.

confirm.delete_message: '{name}' will be removed; its keys keep working but become unattributed.

errors.quota_invalid: Monthly quota must be a plain number, or blank.
errors.end_user_limit_invalid: Per-end-user rate limit must be a plain number, or blank.

errors.quota_invalid: Monthly quota must be a plain number, or blank.
errors.end_user_limit_invalid: Per-end-user rate limit must be a plain number, or blank.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this consumer. Leaving now will discard them.

monitors.title: Monitors
monitors.subtitle: Synthetic uptime + schema-drift checks, replaying saved examples on a schedule.

empty.no_monitors: No tools are monitored yet. Configure a monitor from a tool's settings in Server detail.

errors.load_failed: Failed to load monitors. Check your connection and try again.

policies.title: Guard policies
policies.subtitle: Reusable rate-limit / timeout templates. Apply one to every tool in a bundle at once — each tool's existing API-key allow-list is left untouched.

notice.skipped: {count} tool(s) were skipped because they no longer exist in the registry.

empty.no_policies: No policies yet. A policy applies a rate limit and timeout across every tool at once, instead of setting each one individually.

confirm.delete_message: '{name}' will be removed. Already-applied guards on tools are not reverted.
confirm.apply_message: Applying '{name}' will overwrite the existing rate-limit and timeout guards on every tool in the '{bundle}' bundle.

errors.rate_invalid: Rate limit must be a plain number (no units), or blank.
errors.timeout_invalid: Timeout must be a plain number (no units), or blank.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this policy. Leaving now will discard them.

schedules.title: Maintenance schedules
schedules.subtitle: Automatically enables or disables a client or a single tool on a recurring schedule, evaluated once a minute in UTC on the leader instance.

empty.no_schedules: No schedules yet. A schedule enables or disables a client or tool automatically on a cron interval.

confirm.delete_message: This schedule ({cron}) for {client}{tool_part} will be removed.

new.title: New schedule
new.subtitle: Automatically enables or disables a client or a single tool on a recurring schedule, evaluated once a minute in UTC on the leader instance.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this schedule. Leaving now will discard them.

sso_settings.title: Single sign-on (OIDC)
sso_settings.subtitle: Let admins sign in through an external identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace, Auth0, or any OIDC-compliant IdP) via Authorization Code + PKCE, instead of — or alongside — the built-in username/password login. SAML is not supported.

placeholders.secret_configured: Configured — required again every time you save

hints.issuer_p2: — no need to enter individual endpoints.
hints.client_secret: Write-only: stored encrypted at rest and never shown again once saved — re-enter it on every save, even one that only changes another field below.
hints.redirect_uri: Must exactly match a redirect URI registered with the identity provider.

provisioning.label: New SSO users are provisioned as
provisioning.p1: — always, regardless of any other setting. An existing admin must manually promote a new SSO user from the

sso_settings.enable_label: Enable SSO login (shows a "Sign in with SSO" button on the login page)

errors.required_fields: Issuer, client ID, and redirect URI are required.
errors.secret_required_for_update: Re-enter the client secret to save changes — it's never stored in a way this page can read back.

teams.title: Teams
teams.subtitle: Teams own clients; a team-scoped admin only sees and manages its own team's servers. Super-admins (admin role with no team) manage teams and assign ownership. Assign a client's team from its detail page.
teams.empty: No teams yet. A team groups servers under shared ownership, so operator-role admins only see and manage what's assigned to their team.

confirm.delete_message: Delete team '{name}'? Its servers and users become unowned.

users.title: Users

errors.password_too_short: Password must be at least 12 characters.

confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this user. Leaving now will discard them.

users.last_admin_locked: Cannot remove or demote the last admin.

team.change_locked: Team changes are locked while this user is the last admin.

confirm.delete_message: Delete user {username}? They will no longer be able to sign in.
confirm.role_self: You are about to change your own role.
confirm.role_other: You are about to change {username}'s role from {current} to {next}.
confirm.team_self_warning: You are about to change your own team.

usage.title: Usage
usage.errors_hint: Errors include any non-2xx response or thrown exception at the dispatch layer.
usage.truncated_hint: Showing the top entries — drill in to see the rest.

empty.no_calls: No calls in the selected window.
empty.no_attributed: No calls attributed to a registered API key.

traffic.title: Traffic
traffic.subtitle_p1: Every tool call that hits this admin gateway lands here — both successful and failed.
traffic.subtitle_p2: Filter by client or tool, drill into a record to see the exact request/response, or replay it against the current upstream.
traffic.empty_p1: No traffic in the selected window.
traffic.empty_p2: Adjust the filter or wait for fresh traffic to roll in.

replay.tooltip: Replay this call against the current upstream
replay.note_p1: This replays the call against whatever the server is currently pointing at.

confirm.replay_message: This will re-run the exact same call against the current upstream.

ws_proxy_targets.title: WS proxy targets
ws_proxy_targets.subtitle_p1: Live, bidirectional WebSocket passthrough to a raw backend service, served at
ws_proxy_targets.subtitle_p2: . Distinct from a registered server — a target has no tools.
ws_proxy_targets.empty: No WS proxy targets yet. A target lets MCP tools dispatch over a persistent WebSocket connection to a backend service instead of plain REST.

confirm.delete_message: '{name}' will be removed and any live connections force-closed.
confirm.disconnect_message: '{name}' has {count} live connection(s); disconnecting will force-close all of them and drop any in-flight messages.
confirm.leave_message: You have unsaved details for this WS proxy target. Leaving now will discard them.

errors.load_failed: Failed to load WS proxy targets.
errors.name_and_url_required: Name and backend WebSocket URL are required.
errors.max_connections_invalid: Max connections must be a plain number, or blank.
errors.max_message_bytes_invalid: Max message size must be a plain number, or blank.
errors.idle_timeout_invalid: Idle timeout must be a plain number, or blank.

traces.title: Traces
traces.subtitle_p1: Per-call span timing for a built-in waterfall view — independent of any external OTLP collector. Opt-in
traces.subtitle_p2: on the server) — an empty list can mean either storage is off or there's genuinely nothing recent.

confirm.purge_message: Deletes every persisted span. This cannot be undone.

empty.no_traces: No traces yet — once calls flow, they'll appear here.

not_found.title: Not found
not_found.subtitle: The page you're looking for doesn't exist (or no longer does).

cron.on_day_of_month_at: On day {dom} of the month at {time}

github__list_pull_requests.description: List pull requests for a repo

billing-ops.description: Stripe refunds & invoice lookups for finance

readonly-explorer.description: Safe, read-only slice across every backend

builtin:petstore.description: The canonical OpenAPI sample API — pets, orders, and inventory.

custom:1.description: Reusable template for spinning up a staging CRM registration.

add_pet.description: Add a new pet to the store

triage_issue.description: Search GitHub, then post a Slack summary

refund_and_notify.description: Create a Stripe refund and DM the customer owner
